title: The Snow-Blossoms
content:
But yestereve the winter trees
         Reared leafless, blackly bare,
        Their twigs and branches poignant-marked
         Upon the sunset-flare.

        White-petaled, opens now the dawn,
         And in its pallid glow,
        Revealed, each leaf-lorn, barren tree
         Stands white with flowers of snow.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Clark Ashton Smith, titled "The Snow-Blossoms", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
